title: Valkey Server Benchmark — Experiment Configuration
description: Configuration for a single benchmark experiment comparing valkey-server binaries (local or remote) using amz_valkey-benchmark as the load generator.

iterations.description: Number of iterations to run per server binary

servers.description: Array of servers to benchmark (local binaries or remote endpoints)

servers_directory.description: Directory where isolated per-run server home directories are created. Required when using local-server-info servers.

amz_valkey_benchmark_binary.description: Full path to the amz_valkey-benchmark binary

output_directory.description: Base output directory. A timestamped subdirectory is created per invocation.

experiment_duration_seconds.description: Duration in seconds to run the benchmark load. amz_valkey-benchmark runs with --test-duration and exits cleanly after this duration.

warmup_skip_seconds.description: Number of seconds of initial data to discard from metrics (cold-start filtering). Default: 5

port.description: Default port for local servers to listen on. Remote servers define their own port in remote-server-info.

server.description: A server to benchmark — either a local binary (local-server-info) or a remote endpoint (remote-server-info). Exactly one must be specified.

name.description: Unique human-readable name for this server (used in output directories and graphs)

local-server-info.description: Configuration for a locally-managed server. The orchestrator starts and stops this server for each iteration.

binary.description: Full path to the valkey-server / redis-server binary

args.description: Additional command-line arguments for the server (e.g. ['--save', '', '--appendonly', 'no']). Port is always added automatically.

remote-server-info.description: Configuration for a pre-existing remote server. The orchestrator validates it is reachable and in standalone mode before benchmarking. FLUSHALL is run at the start of each iteration.

endpoint.description: DNS name or IP address of the remote Redis/Valkey server

port.description: Port of the remote server. Default: 6379

key_count.description: Number of random keys (-r parameter for amz_valkey-benchmark). Controls the keyspace size.

data_size_bytes.description: Size of SET values in bytes (-d parameter)

seed.description: Random seed for amz_valkey-benchmark (-r uses this as the range). Default: same as key_count.

clients.description: Client connection configuration. set_clients and get_clients are total TCP connections for SET and GET workloads. The orchestrator computes the number of amz_valkey-benchmark processes internally using max_connections_per_benchmark_process.

max_connections_per_benchmark_process.description: Maximum connections per amz_valkey-benchmark process (-c parameter). Processes are split so each has at most this many connections.

set_clients.description: Total TCP connections for SET workload. Processes are computed as ceil(set_clients / max_connections_per_benchmark_process).

get_clients.description: Total TCP connections for GET workload. Processes are computed as ceil(get_clients / max_connections_per_benchmark_process).

numa.description: NUMA pinning configuration. When enabled, benchmark processes are pinned to the benchmark_node. For local servers, the server process is pinned to the server_node. For remote servers, only benchmark pinning applies.

server_node.description: NUMA node for local valkey-server processes (ignored for remote servers)

benchmark_node.description: NUMA node for the amz_valkey-benchmark processes
